title: Meeting Someone New
description: Make small talk when you first meet someone — the questions they'll ask, and how to answer
location: A social gathering, housewarming, or community event
context: You're at a friend's party, a neighbourhood get-together, or any casual social event. Someone new comes over to chat. These are exactly the questions they'll ask — and your answers from your personal intro scene are the replies you'll need. Shadow this scene and your intro scene together for the best results.
culturalFact: Hongkongers tend to ask 'what do you do?' and 'how long have you been here?' pretty quickly when meeting expats. It's not nosiness — it's how they work out what you have in common. Being ready with natural answers in Cantonese is a genuine conversation-starter.

lines.speaker: them
lines.jyutping: wai3, nei5 hou2! nei5 hai6 san1 lai4 ge3?
lines.english: Hey, hi! Are you new here?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 係，我係 [你的名字]。好高興認識你！
lines.jyutping: hai6, ngo5 hai6 [nei5 ge3 meng2]. hou2 gou1 hing3 jing6 sik1 nei5!
lines.english: Yes, I'm [your name]. Great to meet you!
lines.scenario: This is your opening. Swap [your name] for your own name — your personal intro scene will have this phrase built and ready for you.
lines.usage: Start with 我係 [name] (ngo5 hai6...) then add 好高興認識你 (hou2 gou1 hing3 jing6 sik1 nei5) — 'great to meet you'. Warm and natural.

lines.speaker: them
lines.jyutping: nei5 hai6 bin1 dou6 jan4 gaa3?

replies.romanization: ngo5 hai6 gaa1 naa4 daai6 jan4.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 我係英國人，喺倫敦大嘅。
lines.jyutping: ngo5 hai6 jing1 gwok3 jan4, hai2 leon4 deon1 daai6 ge3.
lines.english: I'm British — I grew up in London.
lines.scenario: The key pattern: 我係 [nationality] 人，喺 [hometown] 大嘅. Your personal intro scene will have your own version of this phrase ready to go.
lines.usage: Swap 英國 (jing1 gwok3) for your nationality and 倫敦 (leon4 deon1) for your hometown. Short, clear, easy.

lines.speaker: them
lines.jyutping: lai4 hoeng1 gong2 gei2 noi6 laa3?
lines.english: How long have you been in Hong Kong?

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: ngo5 lai4 zo2 saam1 nin4 laa3. nei5 ne1?
lines.english: I've been here three years. What about you?
lines.scenario: Answer with your time in HK, then flip it back with 你呢 (nei5 ne1) — 'what about you?' This is a great habit in Cantonese conversation. It keeps things going naturally.
lines.usage: Swap 三年 (saam1 nin4, three years) for your own time: 半年 (bun3 nin4, six months), 五年 (ng5 nin4, five years), 十年 (sap6 nin4, ten years).

lines.speaker: them
lines.cjk: 我係本地人，土生土長喺香港㗎。你住喺邊度？
lines.jyutping: ngo5 hai6 bun2 dei6 jan4, tou2 saang1 tou2 zoeng2 hai2 hoeng1 gong2 gaa3. nei5 zyu6 hai2 bin1 dou6?
lines.english: I'm a local — born and raised in Hong Kong. Where do you live?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 我住喺西環，好近地鐵站。
lines.jyutping: ngo5 zyu6 hai2 sai1 waan4, hou2 kan5 dei6 tit3 zaam6.
lines.english: I live in Sai Wan — right near the MTR.
lines.scenario: Tell them your neighbourhood. Your personal intro scene will have your own district phrase built for you.
lines.usage: The pattern: 我住喺 [district] (ngo5 zyu6 hai2...). Add 好近地鐵 (near the MTR) or 行路去得 (haang4 lou6 heoi3 dak1, walkable) if it's true.

lines.speaker: them
lines.jyutping: nei5 zou6 mat1 je5 gung1 zok3 gaa3?
lines.english: What do you do for work?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 我係老師，喺國際學校教書。
lines.jyutping: ngo5 hai6 lou5 si1, hai2 gwok3 zai3 hok6 haau6 gaau3 syu1.
lines.english: I'm a teacher — I work at an international school.
lines.scenario: This is one of the first things people ask in HK. Your personal intro scene will have your job phrase customised for you.
lines.usage: The pattern: 我係 [job] (ngo5 hai6...). Examples: 我係護士 (nurse, wu6 si6), 我係工程師 (engineer, gung1 cing4 si1), 我係醫生 (doctor, ji1 saang1).

lines.speaker: them
lines.cjk: 你廣東話好叻喎！學咗幾耐㗎？
lines.jyutping: nei5 gwong2 dung1 waa2 hou2 lek1 wo3! hok6 zo2 gei2 noi6 gaa3?
lines.english: Your Cantonese is great! How long have you been learning?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 哪裡哪裡！學咗半年，仲差得遠！
lines.jyutping: naa4 lei5 naa4 lei5! hok6 zo2 bun3 nin4, zung6 caa1 dak1 jyun5!
lines.english: Not at all! I've been learning six months — still a long way to go!
lines.scenario: Hongkongers love to compliment your Cantonese. The reply 哪裡哪裡 (naa4 lei5 naa4 lei5) — literally 'where, where?' — means 'not at all, you're too kind'. Being humble here earns serious respect.
lines.usage: Even if your Cantonese is actually pretty good, being modest in response to compliments is deeply natural in Hong Kong. 哪裡哪裡 is a phrase worth mastering early.

lines.speaker: them
lines.jyutping: jau5 mou5 WhatsApp? bou2 ci4 lyun4 lok3 laa1!
lines.english: Got WhatsApp? Let's keep in touch!

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: jau5 aa3! hou2 gou1 hing3 sik1 dou2 nei5.
lines.english: Of course! Really glad to have met you.
lines.scenario: Swapping WhatsApp in HK is how people stay connected — much more common than business cards. Closing with 好高興識到你 (hou2 gou1 hing3 sik1 dou2 nei5) is warm and genuinely memorable.
lines.usage: 好高興識到你 is a great phrase to end any first meeting. Use it every time — it's sincere, natural, and leaves a great impression.
